game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept:
Embark on an emotional first-person narrative adventure where you control the story—and affect its outcomes—with your real-life blinks. With this innovative technique you will fully immerse yourself in a world of memories, both joyous and heartbreaking, as your whole life flashes before your eyes.

Before Your Eyes is a first person narrative adventure which tells the story of a soul’s journey into the afterlife using a new and innovative form of interaction—your real-life blinks.

The story begins after your death, aboard the ship of a mythical Ferryman tasked with shepherding souls to the afterlife. In order to help you pass on, he must first learn the story of your life. And so, he sends you back to relive your most important moments.



The game uses your webcam to immerse you in this story in a way you've never experienced before. You will learn to control the flow of the narrative with your actual eyes–blinking through precious memories of family, first love, and the rise of an artistic career. But soon the Ferryman will suspect you aren't showing him everything he needs to see. So he will force you deeper into the dark recesses of your most repressed memories, ultimately revealing a heartbreaking truth.

By combining its innovative blink interaction mechanic with a charming world and a fully voice-acted story, Before Your Eyes is a deeply emotional and relatable journey—one about the impossible expectations we place on ourselves and the regrets we carry with us.

An earlier version of the game, titled Close Your, received the Game Developers Choice award at Indiecade and the Student Award at the Independent Game Awards (IGF), before receiving funding through a successful Kickstarter campaign.


CW: Before Your Eyes deals with themes of death, illness and grief
game_info.description: Experience a touching journey through life's most precious memories. You are a soul reliving key moments from your past, but you can only stay in each memory for a limited time. Press SPACE to "blink" and transition between memories, collecting emotional fragments to understand your story. Navigate through childhood, first love, artistic dreams, and heartbreaking truths. Reach the end of your journey before time runs out to find peace in the afterlife.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Navigate through memory scenes (left/right to move, up to interact with objects)
SPACE: Blink to advance through memories or interact with key moments
Z: Hold to reflect on current memory (slows time passage)
ENTER: Start the game
ESC: Pause/Unpause
R: Restart to main menu

TEST_1.test_description: Test basic movement and memory navigation. Verify that the player can move between memory zones and that the blink mechanic works correctly. Ensure time progression and memory transitions function as intended.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Move through each memory zone systematically, collecting fragments and using blinks to progress. Test both horizontal movement and interaction mechanics. Verify that time mechanics work properly when holding reflection key.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Player successfully navigates through multiple memories, collects fragments, and demonstrates all movement capabilities. Time progresses correctly and blink transitions work smoothly. No collision or movement errors occur.
TEST_1.description: Test basic movement and memory navigation. Verify that the player can move between memory zones and that the blink mechanic works correctly. Ensure time progression and memory transitions function as intended.
TEST_1.strategy: Move through each memory zone systematically, collecting fragments and using blinks to progress. Test both horizontal movement and interaction mechanics. Verify that time mechanics work properly when holding reflection key.
TEST_1.expected: Player successfully navigates through multiple memories, collects fragments, and demonstrates all movement capabilities. Time progresses correctly and blink transitions work smoothly. No collision or movement errors occur.

TEST_2.test_description: Test optimal strategy to complete the full narrative journey and achieve victory. This validates the win condition by collecting all necessary memory fragments and reaching the final memory before time expires.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Use optimal pathing through memories, collecting all essential fragments efficiently. Strategic use of reflection mechanic to manage time. Prioritize key memories that advance the narrative. Navigate to the final scene within the time limit.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Player reaches GAME_OVER_WIN state by collecting sufficient fragments and completing the journey. All major memories are experienced. The narrative concludes satisfactorily with the soul finding peace. Time management is demonstrated effectively.

TEST_3.test_description: Test time management failure scenario. Verify that running out of time before completing the journey results in GAME_OVER_LOSE condition. This ensures the challenge mechanic works correctly.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Move slowly, avoid collecting fragments efficiently, waste time in early memories. Do not use the reflection mechanic strategically. Allow the time limit to expire before reaching later critical memories.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Game correctly transitions to GAME_OVER_LOSE when time expires. Player receives appropriate feedback about incomplete journey. The lose condition is clear and fair based on time management failure.

TEST_4.test_description: Test interaction system with memory objects and fragments. Verify that collecting fragments updates score correctly and that interactive objects respond properly to player input.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Systematically interact with all collectible fragments and interactive objects in memories. Test collision detection with various object types. Verify score increments and visual feedback for interactions.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: All fragments are collectible, score updates correctly, collision detection works accurately. Visual and gameplay feedback confirms successful interactions. No objects are missed due to collision issues.
